english: Lex. = nám-yaṅ (nam yang); Pth.: gźar-yaṅ mi (gzhar yang mi) never (Sch. and Schr. prob. incorr.).

fut. of _jál-ba ('jal ba), to weigh, sraṅ-la gźál-bar nús-kyi (srang la gzhal bar nus kyi) if one could weigh with a pair of scales Glr.; gźál-dgos-kyi rdzas (gzhal dgos kyi rdzas) Sch.; 'goods for which duties are to be paid', liable to duty, to custom; gźal-du-méd-pa (gzhal du med pa) imponderable Stg.; immensely much Pth.; immeasurable, incomparable, infinite, vast; gźal méd (gzhal med), gźal-yás (gzhal yas) id.; gźal-med-kaṅ (gzhal med khang), more frq. gźal-yas-kaṅ (gzhal yas khang), also gźal-med-kaṅ-bzaṅ (gzhal med khang bzang) palace, rarely used of human palaces (so Glr. in one passage, when speaking of the house of a Brahmin), mostly of the abode of gods Pth. and elsewh.; also Tibet, in po. language, is called a lha-gnás gźal-yas-kaṅ (lha gnas gzhal yas khang), the heavens with the sun a ṅo-mtsár lhai gźal-yas-kaṅ (ngo mtshar lha'i gzhal yas khang). - gźal-tsád (gzhal tshad) measure, scale, standard Sch.
play, sport, jest, joke Sch., Lex.: glu-gźas (glu gzhas).

1. that from which a thing arises, exists, depends; ground, foundation, original cause, exciting cause (मूल Was. 234); dge-légs tams-cád byuṅ-bai gźi(-ma) (dge legs thams cad byung ba'i gzhi (-ma)) the primitive source of all happiness (is the doctrine of Buddha) Glr.; gźi-skye-méd (gzhi skye med) without origin and birth Mil.; gźir bźág-pa (gzhir bzhag pa) prob.: to use as a foundation Mil., Tar.; *gór-źi* (*gor zhi*) W. cause of delay; má-gźi (ma gzhi) v. as an article of its own sub ma (ma); rtsig-gźi (rtsig gzhi) foundation of a wall Cs.; ñúṅ-gźi (nyung gzhi), lá-gźi (la gzhi) turnips, radishes, left for seed (being the foundations, as it were, of new plants); in gźi-sems-ñíd (gzhi sems nyid), gźi-čós-ñíd (gzhi chos nyid) it prob. stands as an apposition, in the sense of kun-gźi (kun gzhi): the spirit, the primeval cause; in a special sense: the innermost essence, inherent nature; gźí-nas (gzhi nas) actually, opp. to 'apparently' Mil.; fundamental law, statute, gźi cén-po (gzhi chen po) title of a book Was. 264; in certain cases it may be translated by action, v. ruṅ-ba (rung ba) 2, c. - 2. ground, floor, gźi-ma gru-bźi (gzhi ma gru bzhi) a square floor Glr.; stéṅ-gi gźi (steng gi gzhi) the upper base, top-surface Stg. - 3. residence, abode, home, gźi _dzin-pa (gzhi 'dzin pa) to take up one's residence in a place Mil. and elsewh.; gźi _bébs-pa (gzhi 'bebs pa) W. *púb-ce* (*phub ce*) id.; gźi-ma rab cíg póg-nas bźág-go (gzhi ma rab cig phog nas bzhag go) he assigned to him a nice dwelling-place and established him there Glr.; seat, place, čós-gźi (chos gzhi) seat of religion, monastery Tar. and elsewh.; school of religion Tar. 44, 17; gźi cíg-tu skád-cig kyaṅ mi sdód-de (gzhi cig tu skad cig kyang mi sdod de) in no place resting for a moment (the arrow flies towards its goal) Thgy., *źi cíg-tu* (*zhi cig tu*) C. the same as rtse gcig-tu (rtse gcig tu). - 4. in philosophy: axiom, proposition Was. (58); contents, tenor (299); basis, support (273). - 5. Sch.: enmity?? - 6. also źe (zhe) (cf. gźes (gzhes)) a definition of time or of relationship: gźi-ñiṅ (gzhi nying), źe-ñiṅ (zhe nying) two years ago, gźi-més (gzhi mes) great-great-grandfather, gźi-més-mo (gzhi mes mo) great-great-grandmother, gźi-tsá (gzhi tsha) great-great-grandchild Sch. - Comp. gźi-dgón (gzhi dgon) monastery of the place, in or near a village, usually very small and harbouring but a few monks. - gźi-ji-bźin-pa (gzhi ji bzhin pa) a recluse, 'who stays where he is' Burn. I, 310. - gźi-bdág (gzhi bdag) lord of the manor, lord of the soil, may denote a king or noble-man, but gen. it is a local deity, presiding over a certain district, to whom travellers are bound to offer sacrifice, and whom to offend they must carefully avoid.

1 to examine, search, try, rtog- (rtog-) (or brtag- (brtag-)) gźig légs-par gtóṅ-ba (gzhig legs par gtong ba) to select and arrange carefully, e.g. books Pth.; lo daṅ zlá-bar rtog-gźig źib-tu gtóṅ-ba (lo dang zla bar rtog gzhig zhib tu gtong ba) to search minutely as to the day and year Pth.; bsam-gźig gtóṅ-ba (bsam gzhig gtong ba) = bsam-bló gtóṅ-ba (bsam blo gtong ba) to weigh, consider Pth. - 2. fut. of _jig-pa ('jig pa).
to put or lay in order Lex., *źib-źib có-ce(s)* (*zhib zhib co ce(s)*) W., *toṅ-wa* (*tong wa*) C. id.

native place, native country Lex.; yul-gźís (yul gzhis) house, estate, property Mil. = źiṅ-káṅ (zhing khang) paternal estate; pa-gźís (pha gzhis) the father's domicile as inheritance; gźis sgril-ba (gzhis sgril ba) to change one's abode, to remove to another place Sch.; gźís-pa (gzhis pa) a native Sch.; gźís-mad (gzhis mad) family, household, wife, children and
